item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really see how this could be a 303 ripoff, excluding the fact that they're both doors and they both induce fear in some way. 4948 is a feeling of fear of closed doors that gets worse until it just stops, while 303 is just a stalking-like figure that stands before a door and scares you. After writing this, I do see how they're partially similar, but this isn't a ripoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-303 Ripoff. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: T'was ok until I saw the "creepy" door picture. Not so creepy, just funny, now. I suggest you change it to something more appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No idea what this one's going for. Others in the comments have covered the weird containment philosophy and the non-sequitur of the meat doors. Have to -1 this article because of those, even though door2.png is creepy as shit and the article made me feel real dread on this site for the first time in a couple hundred articles. As others have said, it reads as creepypasta (good creepypasta, at least), but completely fails to mesh with how the Foundation writes articles and their general M.O.

I also have no idea how to trigger door2.png in the first place, whether it was just a random chance upon refreshing the page, or something that appeared after a certain amount of time. I just dug into the page source to find it after the comments pointed it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see the ghost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's just me, but it seems like random creepyness just for the sake of creepyness. Had to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, could I have this one exempt from cross linking? I feel that it’s a pretty short narrative and I wouldn’t want to slow it down with links

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Blanking post due to wrong article

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I mean, it succeeds as horror, I guess, but your version of the Foundation didn't even try to contain it or explain it. This is just regular creepypasta where you threw a bunch of unrelated concepts together and then didn't do anything with them. There's no "aha" moment when the reader realizes what's actually going on. It's just a description of the Foundation being bad at their job with a cool graphical trick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
door2.png is a clever ML coding trick, but it can't save the schizophrenic description and the self-defeating "containment procedures."

I mean, seriously? "Leave the door ajar at them harder until the skip takes what it wants and goes back out into the general populace?" Even Destroy Destroy Destroy is better containment than that. That's not even "catch and release," it's "leave the skip a free saucer of milk and never attempt to contain it in any way."

Good creepypasta. Great presentation. Terrible SCP. This is literally the opposite of what we do here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's off to a good start but in the end, there's just not enough explanation. How does it 'spread'? What's considered a 'cure'? At a minimum, I suggest adding an interview with Dr. Warner about the events. It sounded traumatic enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this. You leave out all the right parts. I particularly enjoy Neutralized scps that are still creepy as heck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't seem to put the pieces together on this one, but enjoyed it regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote. Creeped me out in all the right ways, then lost me with the flesh doors at the end. I don't know if there's something I'm not seeing, but the final paragraph seemed like a complete non sequitur to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure I started with door2 and went back to door1? Not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy-crap !

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found door2 after reading Rednose's comment, but I don't understand this at all. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if its my internet but I don't think I'm seeing the same thing as everyone else. No-voting for now, maybe I'll get that woah moment everyone else is getting later.
Edit: found door 2 through the edit option, think I spoiled it for my self but I'm not sure what was supposed to trigger it.
edit 2: o shit i saw it, don't spoil this for yourselves people

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a creepy door lad. The idea and especially the ending are spooky, and I enjoy coding shenanigans in skips, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this type of vague and uncanny horror of having something seemingly normal be creepy. I do think the bit about the flesh doors was too much, since the rest of the article does the job well without actually having anything outright bad happen, just a strong sense of wrongness.

Either way, +1 since I still love horror SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indescribable and random picture.I like it.+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a way to do it without it taking a long time, like in SCP-087 or SCP-895, but it requires having a server where you can host a PHP script (AFAIK) and I really don't have that, so JavaScript is the next best thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops, fixed!

And thanks for the translation. Linking it in the author post now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image block taking a long time to load signals image trickery from the start and kind of spoiled this.

Nonetheless I respect a little subtlety present in something simple. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the image. Also translated to Chinese.

I find a wrong tag.. This page seems to be the only page with the tag "contagious". Should it be contagion?

item.title: Oh god...
item.content:encoded: What's that behind the door?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THE DOOR DOESN'T HAVE HINGES! It's the creepiest door ever.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short and certainly intriguing. I don't think I'm seeing the same thing in the image as everyone else, but…

Regardless, good premise. I'm surprised it isn't a phobia in real life. +1

EDIT: OH FUCK I SAW IT. DOUBLE +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Door is everything. It is all that ever was and all that ever will be. It controls time and space, love and death. It can see into your mind. IT CAN SEE INTO YOUR SOUL.

This was 10 pound of WTF in a 5 pound bag. It doesn't help the the AC in my office made a weird droning noise when I saw the thing in door 2. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is extremely creepy, and it's such a simple idea. The thing you did with the image just drives up the creep fact from 10 to 20.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is right up my alley. Hits my creep buttons in one easy-to-swallow package. Tenouttaten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote because I have no idea what's going on AND I'm not sure if that's because I'm missing something or if there's just nothing going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I- I have no idea what's going on. The Description reads a little off for my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m guessing the flesh has something to do with the family?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay this got to me. You nailed the sense of subtle wrongness here, and that bit of "wait what the fuck" with the flesh doors was a good ending. I was thoroughly unnerved.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I get it? Either way, that picture is nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea, but the photoshop job on door2 is still a bit sloppy, with one of the ears overlapping part of the door. The lighting doesn't feel as off as it did last time, but I may just be seeing things in another light.

Novote for now. I'll upvote when door2's fixed.

EDIT: Oh, this. This is beautiful.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This took a surprisingly long while to get right. This is really the first time I've written horror with the intention for horror from the start, and I hope it comes across well.

Firstly, massive shoutout to Shaggydredlocks for helping my through all the fine hurdles of horror. I think this is way better because of you.

Secondly, thanks to DolphinSlugchugger, FloppyPhoenix, UraniumEmpire, stormbreath and PeppersGhost for crit. I've probably forgotten somebody here, please tell me if I did. Thanks to Communism will win for writing SCP-2808, where the image code came from.

Image source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_of_Hiatt-Stricklin_House_interior_showing_front_office_door._Note_the_four_panel_door,_trim,_wall_paper_and_linoleum,_facing_southwest._-_Hiatt_Property,_House,_West_bank_HABS_ID,8-PLAVI.V,1A-10.tif

Edit: should mention that the image of the dude is just the rabbit guy from SCP-4000

Edit: I would like to bring attention to a Chinese translation by W Asriel

Edit: I would like to bring attention to some more translations:

  • A Japanese translation by Hutu-nohito
  • A French translation by Lyrthill
